howItWorks.intro: This freight class calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Class is determined by density: higher density = lower class = lower cost
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What is freight class?
faq.answer: Freight class (50-500) is an NMFC classification used for LTL shipping rates. Lower classes (higher density) cost less to ship. Class is based on density, handling, liability, and stowability.

faq.question: How does freight class affect shipping cost?
faq.answer: Class 50 (densest) can cost 3-5× less per pound than Class 500 (lightest). Moving from Class 100 to Class 85 can save 10-15% on LTL rates.

faq.question: What if my actual NMFC class differs from density-based?
faq.answer: Some commodities have fixed classes regardless of density (e.g., tires are always Class 77.5). Check the NMFC tariff for your specific commodity. This calculator provides a density-based estimate.
